debug_info.goal:
a : Type
⊢ ∀ (sl : SymList a), sl.fromSL.reverse = SymList.fromSL (Chapter3.SymList.reverse a sl)

proof:
by
  intro sl
  rcases sl with ⟨l1, l2, h⟩
  simp [SymList.fromSL, SymList.reverse]
  <;> rw [h]
  <;> rfl

   Recheck the definitions:
   - `List.toSL l = ⟨l, l.reverse⟩` (as per Lean docs)  
   - `SymList.reverse ⟨as, bs⟩ = ⟨bs, as⟩`  
   - `fromSL ⟨as, bs⟩ = as ++ bs`  
   So for `sl = List.toSL l`, `sl.fromSL = l ++ l.reverse`.  
   Then `sl.reverse.fromSL = l.reverse ++ l`.  
   But `List.reverse sl.fromSL = (l ++ l.reverse).reverse = (l.reverse).reverse ++ l.reverse = l ++ l.reverse`.  
   So the statement becomes `l ++ l.reverse = l.reverse ++ l`, which is false unless `l = []`.

   But in the actual problem, it seems that `SymList.reverse` of `⟨a, b⟩` is `⟨b, a⟩`, and `fromSL ⟨a, b⟩ = a ++ b`, so the statement becomes `(a ++ b).reverse = b ++ a`.

   However, `(a ++ b).reverse = b.reverse ++ a.reverse`, not `b ++ a`.

  simp_all [SymList.reverse, List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_append]
  <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_append])
  <;>
  simp_all [SymList.reverse, List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_append]
  <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_append])
  <;>
  simp_all [SymList.reverse, List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_append]
  <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_append])
  <;>
  simp_all [SymList.reverse, List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_append]
  <;>
  (try decide) <;>
  (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_append])

by
  intro sl
  have h₁ : List.reverse sl.fromSL = sl.reverse.fromSL := by
    induction sl as
    | mk l₁ l₂ h₂ =>
      simp_all [SymList.reverse, List.reverse]
      <;>
      (try { contradiction }) <;>
      (try { linarith }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [List.reverse, List.append_assoc, List.append_comm, List.append_left_comm]
        <;>
        (try { linarith })
        <;>
        (try {
          rcases l₁ with (_ | _ | _) <;> simp_all [List.reverse, List.append_assoc, List.append_comm, List.append_left_comm]
          <;>
          (try { linarith })
        })
      })
      <;>
      (try {
        rcases l₁ with (_ | _ | _ | _) <;>
        rcases l₂ with (_ | _ | _ | _) <;>
        simp_all [List.reverse, List.append_assoc, List.append_comm, List.append_left_comm]
        <;>
        (try { linarith })
        <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [List.reverse, List.append_assoc, List.append_comm, List.append_left_comm]
          <;>
          (try { linarith })
        })
      })
  exact h₁

by
  intro sl
  rcases sl with ⟨as, bs, h⟩
  simp_all [SymList.fromSL, List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, SymList.reverse]
  <;> induction as <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]
  <;> try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]
  <;> try aesop
  <;> try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]
  <;> try aesop
  <;> try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]
  <;> try aesop

by
  intro sl
  induction sl with
  | mk l r h =>
    simp [SymList.reverse, List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil]
    <;>
    (try cases r <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]) <;>
    (try cases l <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]) <;>
    (try exact List.reverse_append _ _) <;>
    (try exact by simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]) <;>
    (try rw [← List.append_assoc]) <;>
    (try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]) <;>
    (try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]) <;>
    (try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc])
    <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]
        <;>
        (try cases l <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]) <;>
        (try cases r <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc])
      })
    <;>
    (try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc])
    <;>
    (try

        simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc]

        (try cases r <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc])

    <;>
    (try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.reverse_nil, List.append_assoc])

   Alternatively, perhaps `List.toSL xs = ⟨xs.reverse, xs, _⟩`. 

   Then for `xs = [1,2,3]`, `List.toSL xs = ⟨[3,2,1], [1,2,3], _⟩`.

   - `fromSL` of that is `[3,2,1]` (if it takes the `right` component)
   - Which matches `#eval`. 

   Now, `List.toSL xs = ⟨xs.reverse, xs, _⟩`, and `fromSL` takes the `right` component, then for general `sl = ⟨as, bs, h⟩`:
   - `sl.fromSL = bs`
   - `sl.reverse.fromSL = as`
   - The goal becomes `bs.reverse = as`.

   However, if `List.toSL xs = ⟨xs.reverse, xs, _⟩`, then `as = xs.reverse`, `bs = xs`, and indeed `bs.reverse = xs.reverse = as`. But for general `sl`, we would have `bs.reverse = as`, which cannot be deduced from `as.length = bs.length`.

  intro sl

    intro a l
    simp [SymList.reverse, SymList.toSL, SymList.fromSL]
    <;>
    (try cases l <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.append_assoc]) <;>
    (try induction l <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.append_assoc]) <;>
    (try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.append_assoc]) <;>
    (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.append_assoc])
    <;>
    (try
      {
        simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.append_assoc]
        <;>
        (try ring_nf at *) <;>
        (try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.append_assoc])
      }
    )
  have h₁ := h (sl.fromSL)
  simp_all [SymList.reverse]
  <;>
  (try simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.append_assoc])
  <;>
  (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all [List.reverse_cons, List.append_assoc])
